title: Merge multiple pdfs and Photos into one pdf
excerpt: It would be helpful if it was possible to turn multiple files in the file column into one pdf

posts.author: Doughty94 (Darrin Doughty)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be helpful if it was possible to turn multiple files in the file column into one pdf

posts.author: johnsmithe34 (John Smith)
posts.cookedHtml:
I understand you’d like to combine multiple files into one PDF. Here are some methods you can use, depending on your preference:

Using Online Tools:

  • Free online tools: Several free online tools like PDFUP Comprimi PDF - Riduci le dimensioni del PDF online allow you to upload and merge multiple files into a single PDF. These are convenient as they work directly from your web browser and don’t require software installation.

Using Desktop Software:

  • Adobe Acrobat Pro: This paid software offers a robust suite of PDF management tools, including merging PDFs. You can arrange the order of the files before creating the final merged document.
  • PDFelement Pro: A paid alternative with similar functionalities to Adobe Acrobat Pro, allowing you to combine and rearrange PDFs before saving them as a single file.
  • LibreOffice Draw: This free and open-source software can also open and edit PDFs, including merging them into one document. While not as feature-rich as paid options, it’s a good choice for basic needs.

Using Printing Options:

  • While not the most efficient method, some PDF readers like Adobe Acrobat Reader allow you to “Print” multiple PDFs as a single document. This involves selecting all the desired PDFs, choosing a “Print to PDF” option (if available) and specifying a new output file name for the merged document.

Remember, different methods have limitations. Free online tools might have file size or usage restrictions. Paid software offers more features but requires purchasing a license.

Choose the method that best suits your needs, considering factors like file size, desired features, and budget.

posts.author: Flowtech (Flowtech Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @darrin

If you’re looking for an easy way to create customized documents (like PDFs, invoices, quotes, reports, or contracts) directly from your monday.com data, you might want to check out DocCreate.

With DocCreate, you can:

  • Automatically generate documents using your board data
  • Customize layouts with your branding, logos, headers, and footers
  • Embed images directly from your boards for richer, more visual documents
  • Pull data from multiple columns, connected boards, or subitems
  • Save generated documents directly into file columns on your boards
  • Automate document creation triggered by item updates or form submissions

It’s a great way to eliminate manual exports, double entry, and formatting headaches — all within your monday workflow.
